draft.title: Manchester's population reached 115,644 in 2020 as city evolves
draft.headline: Manchester's population reaches 115,644 in latest census
draft.subheadline: Southern New Hampshire University now serves over 200,000 learners worldwide.
draft.metaDescription: Manchester, NH, known as the Queen City, had a population of 115,644 in 2020. Southern New Hampshire University serves over 200,000 learners worldwide.
draft.bodyHtml:
Manchester, known as the Queen City, had a population of 115,644 at the 2020 census. The city has a land area of 33.07 square miles and a rich history dating back to its incorporation in 1751.

Under the leadership of Mayor Jay Ruais, the city government includes a diverse group of aldermen such as Bryce Kaw-Uh and Christine Fajardo. Their focus is on maintaining the city's infrastructure while fostering community engagement.

Southern New Hampshire University enrolls over 200,000 learners

Education plays a crucial role in Manchester's development. Southern New Hampshire University, a key institution, serves more than 200,000 learners worldwide, contributing to the local economy and workforce.

Manchester's geographical coordinates are 42.99083°N latitude and -71.46361°W longitude, positioning it as a central hub in New Hampshire. The city is not only known for its educational institutions but also for its vibrant community and historical significance.
